antipattern.description: Multi-class router across haiku/sonnet/opus — calibration becomes intractable.
antipattern.reason: Binary keeps the decision tractable; chain binary routers for tiered cases (r1).

antipattern.description: Labels from a single LLM judge — biases propagate into routing.
antipattern.reason: Use human preference or LLM-jury (3+ judges, majority vote) (r2).

antipattern.description: No uncertainty band — close calls route to weak; hard prompts fail silently.
antipattern.reason: Set uncertainty_band (default 0.1); fall back to strong when crossing (r4).

antipattern.description: No drift monitoring — router stays in production for months while user mix shifts.
antipattern.reason: Sample N% of routed prompts; jury vs router agreement; retrain when agreement < drift_threshold (r5).

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Before deploy, owner reviews: (a) jury labels (not single judge) drive training; (b) calibration plot shows strong-accuracy regression <= 5%; (c) drift monitor scheduled with on-call wired.
